sections.label: Section 1282
sections.original: ὁρῶ μέγιστον ἄλγος ἡ τάλαινʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: I see the greatest grief , wretched that I am .

sections.label: Section 1283
sections.original: μῶν σοι λέοντι φαίνεται προσεικέναι ;
sections.translation: Does it seem to you to be like a lion ?

sections.label: Section 1284
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ Πενθέως ἡ τάλαινʼ ἔχω κάρα .
sections.translation: No , but I , wretched , hold the head of Pentheus .

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.original: ᾠμωγμένον γε πρόσθεν ἢ σὲ γνωρίσαι .
sections.translation: Yes , much lamented before you recognized him .

sections.label: Section 1286
sections.original: τίς ἔκτανέν νιν ;— πῶς ἐμὰς ἦλθεν χέρας ;
sections.translation: Who killed him ? How did he come into my hands ?

sections.label: Section 1287
sections.original: δύστηνʼ ἀλήθειʼ , ὡς ἐν οὐ καιρῷ πάρει .
sections.translation: Miserable truth , how inopportunely you arrive !

sections.label: Section 1288
sections.original: λέγʼ , ὡς τὸ μέλλον καρδία πήδημʼ ἔχει .
sections.translation: Tell me . My heart leaps at what is to come .

sections.label: Section 1289
sections.original: σύ νιν κατέκτας καὶ κασίγνηται σέθεν .
sections.translation: You and your sisters killed him .

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.original: ποῦ δʼ ὤλετʼ ; ἦ κατʼ οἶκον ; ἢ ποίοις τόποις ;
sections.translation: Where did he die ? Was it here at home , or in what place ?

sections.label: Section 1291
sections.original: οὗπερ πρὶν Ἀκτέωνα διέλαχον κύνες .
sections.translation: Where formerly dogs divided Actaeon among themselves .

sections.label: Section 1292
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐς Κιθαιρῶνʼ ἦλθε δυσδαίμων ὅδε ;
sections.translation: And why did this ill-fated man go to Kithairon ?

sections.label: Section 1293
sections.original: ἐκερτόμει θεὸν σάς τε βακχείας μολών .
sections.translation: He went to mock the god and your revelry .

sections.label: Section 1294
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δʼ ἐκεῖσε τίνι τρόπῳ κατήραμεν ;
sections.translation: But in what way did we go there ?

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.original: ἐμάνητε , πᾶσά τʼ ἐξεβακχεύθη πόλις .
sections.translation: You were mad , and the whole city was frantic with Bacchus .

sections.label: Section 1296
sections.original: Διόνυσος ἡμᾶς ὤλεσʼ , ἄρτι μανθάνω .
sections.translation: Dionysus destroyed us — now I understand .

sections.label: Section 1297
sections.original: ὕβριν γʼ ὑβρισθείς · θεὸν γὰρ οὐχ ἡγεῖσθέ νιν .
sections.translation: Being insulted with insolence , for you did not consider him a god .

sections.label: Section 1298
sections.original: τὸ φίλτατον δὲ σῶμα ποῦ παιδός , πάτερ ;
sections.translation: And where is the body of my dearest child , father ?

sections.label: Section 1299
sections.original: ἐγὼ μόλις τόδʼ ἐξερευνήσας φέρω .
sections.translation: I have traced it with difficulty and brought it back .

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.original: ἦ πᾶν ἐν ἄρθροις συγκεκλῃμένον καλῶς ;
sections.translation: Are its joints laid properly together ?

sections.label: Section 1301
sections.original: Πενθεῖ δὲ τί μέρος ἀφροσύνης προσῆκʼ ἐμῆς ;
sections.translation: What part did Pentheus have in my folly ?

sections.label: Section 1302
sections.original: ὑμῖν ἐγένεθʼ ὅμοιος , οὐ σέβων θεόν .
sections.translation: He , like you , did not revere the god , who therefore joined all in one ruin , both you and this one here , and thus destroyed the house and me ,

sections.label: Section 1305
sections.original: κἄμʼ , ὅστις ἄτεκνος ἀρσένων παίδων γεγὼς
sections.translation: , who am bereft of my male children and see this offspring of your womb , wretched woman , most miserably and shamefully slain . He was the hope of our line — you , child , who supported the house , son of my daughter ,

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.original: πόλει τε τάρβος ἦσθα · τὸν γέροντα δὲ
sections.translation: an object of fear to the city ; seeing you , no one wished to insult the old man , for you would have given a worthy punishment . But now I , great Kadmos , who sowed and reaped

sections.label: Section 1315
sections.original: ἔσπειρα κἀξήμησα κάλλιστον θέρος .
sections.translation: a most glorious crop , the Theban people , will be banished from the house without honor . Dearest of men — for though you are dead I still count you among my dearest , child — no longer will you embrace me , calling me grandfather , touching my chin with your hand , child , and

sections.label: Section 1320
sections.original: λέγων · Τίς ἀδικεῖ , τίς σʼ ἀτιμάζει , γέρον ;
sections.translation: saying : Who wrongs you , old man , who dishonors you ? Who vexes and troubles your heart ? Tell me , father , so that I can punish the one who does you wrong . But now I am miserable , while you are wretched , your mother is pitiful , and wretched too are your relatives .

sections.label: Section 1325
sections.original: εἰ δʼ ἔστιν ὅστις δαιμόνων ὑπερφρονεῖ ,
sections.translation: If anyone scorns the gods , let him look to the death of this man and acknowledge them .

sections.label: Section 1327
sections.original: τὸ μὲν σὸν ἀλγῶ , Κάδμε · σὸς δʼ ἔχει δίκην
sections.translation: I grieve for you , Kadmos . Your daughter ’ s child has a punishment deserved indeed , but grievous to you .

sections.label: Section 1329
sections.original: ὦ πάτερ , ὁρᾷς γὰρ τἄμʼ ὅσῳ μετεστράφη ⋯
sections.translation: Father , for you see how much my situation has changed ... ⋯

sections.label: Section 1330
sections.original: δράκων γενήσῃ μεταβαλών , δάμαρ τε σὴ
sections.translation: . . . changing your form , you will become a dragon , and your wife , Harmonia , Ares ’ daughter , whom you though mortal held in marriage , will be turned into a beast , and will receive in exchange the form of a serpent . And as the oracle of Zeus says , you will drive along with your wife a chariot of heifers , ruling over barbarians .

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.original: πολλὰς δὲ πέρσεις ἀναρίθμῳ στρατεύματι
sections.translation: You will sack many cities with a force of countless numbers . And when they plunder the oracle of Apollo , they will have a miserable return , but Ares will protect you and Harmonia and will settle your life in the land of the blessed .

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.original: ταῦτʼ οὐχὶ θνητοῦ πατρὸς ἐκγεγὼς λέγω
sections.translation: That is what I , Dionysus , born not from a mortal father , but from Zeus , say . And if you had known how to be wise when you did not wish to be , you would have acquired Zeus ’ son as an ally , and would now be happy .

sections.label: Section 1344
sections.original: Διόνυσε , λισσόμεσθά σʼ , ἠδικήκαμεν .
sections.translation: Dionysus , we beseech you , we have acted injustly .

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.original: ὄψʼ ἐμάθεθʼ ἡμᾶς , ὅτε δὲ χρῆν , οὐκ ᾔδετε .
sections.translation: You have learned it too late ; you did not know it when you should have .

sections.label: Section 1346
sections.original: ἐγνώκαμεν ταῦτʼ · ἀλλʼ ἐπεξέρχῃ λίαν .
sections.translation: Now we know , but you go too far against us .

sections.label: Section 1347
sections.original: καὶ γὰρ πρὸς ὑμῶν θεὸς γεγὼς ὑβριζόμην .
sections.translation: Yes , for I , a god by birth , was insulted by you .

sections.label: Section 1348
sections.original: ὀργὰς πρέπει θεοὺς οὐχ ὁμοιοῦσθαι βροτοῖς .
sections.translation: Gods should not resemble mortals in their anger .

sections.label: Section 1349
sections.original: πάλαι τάδε Ζεὺς οὑμὸς ἐπένευσεν πατήρ .
sections.translation: My father Zeus approved this long ago .

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.original: αἰαῖ , δέδοκται , πρέσβυ , τλήμονες φυγαί .
sections.translation: Alas ! A miserable exile has been decreed for us , old man .

sections.label: Section 1351
sections.original: τί δῆτα μέλλεθʼ ἅπερ ἀναγκαίως ἔχει ;
sections.translation: Why then do you delay what must necessarily be ?

sections.label: Section 1352
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον , ὡς ἐς δεινὸν ἤλθομεν κακὸν
sections.translation: Child , what a terrible disaster we have all come to — unhappy you , your sisters , and unhappy me . I shall reach a foreign land

sections.label: Section 1355
sections.original: γέρων μέτοικος · ἔτι δέ μοι τὸ θέσφατον
sections.translation: as an aged immigrant . Still it is foretold that I shall bring into Hellas a motley barbarian army . Leading their spears , I , having the fierce nature of a serpent , will bring my wife Harmonia , daughter of Ares , to the altars and tombs of Hellas .

sections.label: Section 1360
sections.original: ἡγούμενος λόγχαισιν · οὐδὲ παύσομαι
sections.translation: I will neither rest from my troubles in my misery , nor will I sail over the downward flowing Acheron and be at peace .

sections.label: Section 1363
sections.original: ὦ πάτερ , ἐγὼ δὲ σοῦ στερεῖσα φεύξομαι .
sections.translation: O father , I will go into exile deprived of you .

sections.label: Section 1364
sections.original: τί μʼ ἀμφιβάλλεις χερσίν , ὦ τάλαινα παῖ ,
sections.translation: Why do you embrace me with your hands , child ,

sections.label: Section 1365
sections.original: ὄρνις ὅπως κηφῆνα πολιόχρων κύκνος ;
sections.translation: like a swan for its exhausted gray-haired parent ?

sections.label: Section 1366
sections.original: ποῖ γὰρ τράπωμαι πατρίδος ἐκβεβλημένη ;
sections.translation: For where can I turn , banished from my father-land ?

sections.label: Section 1367
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα , τέκνον · μικρὸς ἐπίκουρος πατήρ .
sections.translation: I do not know , child ; your father is a poor ally .

sections.label: Section 1368
sections.original: χαῖρʼ , ὦ μέλαθρον , χαῖρʼ , ὦ πατρία
sections.translation: Farewell , house , farewell , city of my forefathers . In misfortune I leave you ,

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: φυγὰς ἐκ θαλάμων .
sections.translation: a fugitive from my chamber .

sections.label: Section 1371
sections.original: στεῖχέ νυν , ὦ παῖ , τὸν Ἀρισταίου ⋯
sections.translation: Go now , child , to the land of Aristaeus . . . ⋯

sections.label: Section 1372
sections.original: στένομαί σε , πάτερ .
sections.translation: I grieve for you , father .

sections.label: Section 1372b
sections.original: κἀγὼ σέ , τέκνον ,
sections.translation: And I for you , child , and I weep for your sisters .

sections.label: Section 1374
sections.original: δεινῶς γὰρ τάνδʼ αἰκείαν
sections.translation: Terribly indeed has

sections.label: Section 1375
sections.original: Διόνυσος ἄναξ τοὺς σοὺς εἰς
sections.translation: lord Dionysus brought this misery to your home .

sections.label: Section 1377
sections.original: καὶ γὰρ ἔπασχον δεινὰ πρὸς ὑμῶν ,
sections.translation: Yes , for I suffered terrible things at your hands , with my name not honored in Thebes .

sections.label: Section 1379
sections.original: χαῖρε , πάτερ , μοι .
sections.translation: Farewell , my father .

sections.label: Section 1379b
sections.original: χαῖρʼ , ὦ μελέα
sections.translation: Farewell , unhappy

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: θύγατερ . χαλεπῶς δʼ ἐς τόδʼ ἂν ἥκοις .
sections.translation: daughter ; and yet you cannot easily fare well .

sections.label: Section 1381
sections.original: ἄγετʼ , ὦ πομποί , με κασιγνήτας
sections.translation: Lead me , escorts , where I may take my pitiful sisters as companions to my exile . May I go where accursed Kithairon may not see me ,
